service.name: HTTP Proxy
service.informativeText:
Abstract: This service allows a Client device, typically a sensor, to communicate with a Web Server through a gateway device. The gateway device implements the HTTP Proxy Service and therefore provides the services available through the Internet to the Client sensor device.
Summary: The HTTP Proxy Service (HPS) allows a device to expose HTTP Web Services to a client. This enables an HPS client device to program a set of characteristics that configures a Hyper Text Transfer Protocol (HTTP) request, initiate this, request, and then read the response.

characteristic.name: URI
characteristic.informativeText: Abstract: The Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) Characteristic is used to configure the URI for a subsequent request.

characteristic.name: HTTP Headers
characteristic.informativeText: Abstract: The HTTP Headers Characteristic is used to hold the headers that would be sent to the HTTP Request or the headers contained within an HTTP response message from the HTTP Server.

characteristic.name: HTTP Entity Body
characteristic.informativeText: Abstract: The HTTP Entity Body Characteristic contains the contents of the message body after any Transfer Encoding has been applied.

characteristic.name: HTTP Control Point
characteristic.informativeText: Abstract: The HTTP Control Point is used to initiate a request to send an HTTP request message from the device contgaining the HTTP Proxy Service, acting as an HTTP Client, and an HTTP Server.

characteristic.name: HTTP Status Code
characteristic.informativeText: Abstract: The HTTP Status Code characteristic contains the Status-Code from the Status-Line of the first line of the HTTP Response Message, followed by one octet indicating the Data Status Bit Field indicating the status of the data received.

characteristic.name: HTTPS Security
characteristic.informativeText: Abstract: The HTTPS Security characteristic contains the known authenticity of the HTTPS Server certificate for the URI.
